name: Chengtu Chicken - Szechuan
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into 1 inch cubes.
2. Mix marinade ingredients in a medium bowl and add chicken, mixing well. Let stand for 20 minutes.
3. Mix seasoning sauce ingredients in a small cup and set aside.
4. Heat 1 cup oil in a wok over medium heat for 1 minute.
5. Stir-fry marinated chicken cubes until very lightly browned, about 1 minute. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain over the wok. Set aside.
6. Remove oil from wok except for 2 tablespoons.
7. Heat remaining oil in wok over medium heat for 30 seconds.
8. Add spinach and salt; stir-fry until spinach is tender, about 1 minute. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain over the wok. Arrange around the outside of a warm platter.
9. Add 3 tablespoons of oil to wok and heat over medium heat for 1 minute.
10. Add ginger, garlic, green onion, and hot bean sauce; stir-fry until fragrant, about 1 to 2 minutes.
11. Add chicken cubes and seasoning sauce; stir-fry until sauce thickens slightly.
12. Add 1 teaspoon sesame oil to wok and stir to mix.
13. Place the chicken mixture in the middle of the platter with spinach. Makes 4 servings.
1. Heat a medium saucepan over medium-low heat for 1 minute.
2. Add peppercorns and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and let cool.
4. Grind peppercorns to a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or pepper mill.
5. Add salt, mix well, and store in a tightly covered container. Makes about 1/4 cup.
